title: I1C deterministic scenario suites and harness remediation evaluation
acceptance_criteria: scenario-suite v1 documentation defines strict fields, repository-relative scenario paths, expected outcomes, expected constraint-code ordering, suite pass/fail semantics, deterministic JSON output, non-goals, and limitations
suite runner executes existing resource scenarios without changing resource-scenario or resource-transition semantics
valid completed and expected constraint_violation suites exit 0 while malformed suites, invalid scenarios, missing scenarios, unsupported options, and expectation mismatches exit nonzero

canonical .\eng.ps1 verify covers suite contract validation, suite tests, representative suite execution, deterministic suite-output hashing, expected mismatch failure, and status capability honesty without dependencies, providers, adapters, KB, or network calls
installed harness at HARNESS_EVALUATION_SHA generates external evidence outside both source checkouts without editable installation, PYTHONPATH, or bespoke evidence-generation scripts

integrity, relocation, tamper, missing-artifact, target-mutation, review-supplement, review-bundle, candidate-lifecycle, reviewer, stale-binding, and final-head tests are recorded honestly under ignored artifacts

status metadata reports scenario suite contract and runner only when implemented while simulation, scheduler, Bitcoin workload, AI workload, network, provider, adapter, hardware, and mission-authority capabilities remain false
product and harness conclusions remain separate; incubation evidence is not operational adoption; no candidate is verified automatically and no canonical KB write occurs
